title: Violations may cost Alyeska $817,000
summary: Federal regulators have proposed $817,000 in fines against an Anchorage company for safety violations along the trans-Alaska oil pipeline. The civil penalty stems from two incidents early last year: a fire at Pump Station 9 near Delta Junction, and a 900-gallon crude oil spill near Coldfoot. A third incident in which a cleaning device called a scraper pig disintegrated inside the pipe also drew the scrutiny of inspectors with the Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration. Chris Hoidal, the agency's western region director, said Wednesday the fire and spill were considered "high-risk events" warranting steep fines. No one was injured in the incidents, but Hoidal said "the potential consequences could have been large." Alyeska Pipeline Service Co., the Anchorage-based oil-compan…

title: Thursday's oil, gas, gold and zinc prices
summary: Today's oil, gas, gold and zinc prices The price of North Slope oil sold on West Coast open markets closed today at $88.43 per barrel, down 71 cents from Wednesday's price. The U.S. natural gas price at the Lower 48's Henry Hub closed today at $8.10 per million BTU, down 12 cents for the day. Gold prices closed at $879.50 per ounce, down $1.10 for the day on the New York Mercantile Exchange. Zinc prices closed at $1.0706 per pound, down 3.02 cents for the day.

title: Women testify about escort business
summary: The girls and women in Jerry Starr's escort business had rules. Look good. No disrespecting the man in charge. And no buying drugs from someone else. That could get you slapped around or worse. That was the testimony Thursday of two young women who used to work for Starr. Starr's real name is Don Arthur Webster Jr. and he's being tried in U.S. District Court on 37 charges: 16 counts related to sex trafficking and 21 drug offenses. Webster, 51, has pleaded not guilty. What's expected to be a three-week trial began Monday in Anchorage. He's accused of luring women and girls into the Anchorage sex trade, then keeping them there with cocaine, threats and beatings. One testified Thursday that her face was scarred from where he hit her. "It busted open, right here," she said, pointing to her rig…

Twenty-year-old Justin continues to struggle with fetal alcohol syndrome and a host of medical problems, including cerebral palsy and severe vision and hearing impairment. title: Skier recalls ride in a 'tidal wave' slide
summary: For a moment, there was only suffocating pressure, darkness and cold. Rory Stark struggled to get free, but couldn't move underneath the mountain of snow. He was buried alive. Stark, 36, was backcountry skiing in Chugach National Forest with five friends when he and Tyler Johnson, 31, climbed near the top of Silvertip Mountain to ski the ridge line just after noon last Saturday. The slope was steep and icy, and the men had to take off their skis to kick-step their way up the mountain. Aware of the avalanche danger in the area, the experienced skiers decided to skirt the ridge line to avoid triggering a slide. Johnson went first and made it about 500 yards before stopping to wait for his friend. He turned and watched Stark ski down, carefully staying about 50 yards away from the tracks John…

title: Ex-beauty queen accused of kidnapping
summary: A law school student and former beauty queen who has posed for a racy calendar while brandishing a weapon has been accused of kidnapping, biting and threatening a former boyfriend with a handgun. Kumari Fulbright, 25, who is midway through her second year in law school, faces a long prison term if convicted of kidnapping, armed robbery, aggravated robbery and two counts of aggravated assault. Fulbright, who competed for the Miss Arizona title in 2005 and 2006, recently completed a semester-long unpaid stint clerking for a federal judge, U.S. District Judge Raner Collins, his office said. She also poses wearing a shiny black bikini in a 2008 calendar that features women holding guns. In the Dec. 18 indictment, Fulbright is accused of holding and torturing her 24-year-old ex-boyfriend in ear…

title: Checks end Alaska's top investment scam
summary: This week Larry Compton aims to mail out nearly 700 checks totaling more than $1.2 million. It's the final step in the epic Raejean Bonham bankruptcy case. The last act in Alaska's biggest investment scam ever. The coda to a money meltdown that burned hundreds of investors in 42 states, not to mention Puerto Rico and Kenya. They're the people getting the checks -- a painfully partial reimbursement for their attempt to get rich quick. "At the end of the day, they will say it was too good to be true -- I was just greedy," said Compton, the Anchorage trustee appointed by a judge to sort out the mess. "Every one of them should have known better." Several investors contacted this week declined to talk publicly about their involvement with Bonham and her Fairbanks travel business, World Plus Inc…

title: Metcalfe: McGuire lobbied and bribed
summary: U.S. Senate candidate Ray Metcalfe filed a complaint Friday with the Alaska Public Offices Commission that claims state Sen. Lesil McGuire's work as a consultant for Providence Health System amounted to "lobbying and bribery." It's the latest in a series of accusations that Metcalfe has leveled against McGuire, an Anchorage Republican. Her lawyer, Charles Dunnagan, said that the claim is ridiculous and Metcalfe's previous accusations have been dismissed. "It is easy to bandy about these ugly, ugly allegations," Dunnagan said. McGuire described her 2003 work for Providence on state financial disclosure forms as "consulting and research." Metcalfe, who lost to McGuire in a 2000 race for the state House of Representatives, alleges in his written complaint that it was something more. "I believ…

title: High-speed chase ends in arrest  of the wrong guy
summary: A man who sped out of a liquor store parking lot just minutes after it was robbed at gunpoint led police on a high-speed chase that ended with officers laying down a spike strip, ramming his car off the street and arresting him, according to Anchorage police. Rory Elsdon, 28, is facing two felony counts, but not for robbing the store. "Turns out he's not the suspect, but he freaked out because he had drugs," police Lt. Paul Honeman said. "He was driving like a madman. This guy obviously needed to be off the road." While Elsdon is apparently not the robber who held up the liquor store Wednesday night, police and the FBI are continuing their search for what appears to be one man who robbed it and two banks in the past two weeks. The most recent robbery came Thursday, when a gun-wielding man …
